title: Bumelia peckhamensis
intro: Bumelia peckhamensis, also known as the Caribbean numbing wood or numbing bumelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. The health benefits of Bumelia peckhamensis include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat pain, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bumelia peckhamensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to make topical creams and ointments for pain relief.
taxonomy: Bumelia peckhamensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Bumelia, which is a group of tropical and subtropical trees. Bumelia peckhamensis is native to Central America and is also known as the "Central American Bumelia".
